ocr.text: The tanker spot market in the Asia-Pacific region has exhibited significant fluctuations in recent quarters, influenced by varying
demand dynamics and geopolitical factors impacting global trade routes. As indicated in the accompanying data table, the first
quarter of 2024 (Q12024) recorded a SpotRate(CNY) of 150.0, reflecting the ongoing volatility in shipping costs amidst rising
operational expenses and supply chain disruptions. This increase in spot rates can be attributed to a robust recovery in domestic
consumption, evidenced by the DomesticMarket share, which stood at 25.0% during the same period. Such a performance
underscores the importance of local demand in stabilizing the tanker market, particularly as international trade continues to face

headwinds. 

Furthermore, the data highlights the ExportMarket, which saw a total volume of 2100 during Q22024, indicating a
strong rebound in export activity as manufacturers ramped up production to meet global demand. This increase has undoubtedly
contributed to heightened competition among shipping companies vying for a larger MarketShare(%), as they respond to the
evolving landscape of both domestic and international markets. The percentage of MarketShare in the Asia-Pacific region
during Q32024 reached 77.0%, showcasing the region's pivotal role in the global tanker industry.

Quarter  SpotRate(CNY)  YoYChange(%)  Asia-Pacific  MarketShare(%)  TotalVolume  ExportMarket  MarketShare(%)  DomesticMarket
Overall, the interplay between domestic and export markets, alongside the varying spot rates, paints a complex picture of the
tanker shipping landscape in the Asia-Pacific. Stakeholders must remain vigilant and adaptive to these changes, as they will
likely continue to impact shipping strategies and pricing mechanisms in the coming quarters. The data presented in this report
provides critical insights for industry participants seeking to navigate this challenging environment, emphasizing the need for
strategic foresight and operational flexibility.
